add_item.title: Add Item
add_item.fill_all_fields: Please fill in title, body, and choose an image.

how_this_works.heading: How it works
how_this_works.subtitle: A quick guide to browsing and reserving from the Activist Resource Library.

browse.title: Browse the inventory
browse.body: Use the Equipment, Books, and Rooms filters to find what you need. Each card shows whether an item is available today, needs a closer look on the calendar, or is already reserved.

account.title: Create a member account
account.body: Register with your email and password, then read and agree to the member agreement. You need an account before you can submit a reservation request.

reserve.title: Pick your dates
reserve.body: Click Reserve Inventory on an item to open the calendar. Equipment and books reserve in fixed Tuesday blocks (one week or one month). Rooms use a flexible date range.

approval.title: Wait for confirmation
approval.body: Your request is submitted as pending. Apathy is Boring reviews it and emails you when it is approved or declined. Pending dates are held on the calendar until a decision is made.

pickup.title: Pick up and return
pickup.body: For equipment and books, pickup and drop-off are on Tuesdays between 10am and 5pm at the Apathy is Boring office. Return items on time so other members can use them.

items.text: Do your little bit of good where you are; it's those little bits of good put together that overwhelm the world.
items.author: Desmond Tutu

items.text: Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world; indeed, it's the only thing that ever has.
items.author: Margaret Mead

items.text: I am only one, but still I am one. I cannot do everything, but still I can do something; and because I cannot do everything, I will not refuse to do something that I can do.
items.author: Helen Keller

items.text: What you do makes a difference, and you have to decide what kind of difference you want to make.
items.author: Jane Goodall

items.text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
items.author: Nelson Mandela

items.text: The most common way people give up their power is by thinking they don't have any.
items.author: Alice Walker

items.text: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.
items.author: Arthur Ashe

items.text: When the whole world is silent, even one voice becomes powerful.
items.author: Malala Yousafzai

items.text: The time is always right to do what is right.
items.author: Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

items.text: When I was a boy and I would see scary things in the news, my mother would say to me, 'Look for the helpers. You will always find people who are helping.'
items.author: Fred Rogers
